note: Mathlib/Analysis/Convex/DoublyStochasticMatrix.lean:42 -- def doublyStochastic (R n) [Fintype n] ... : Submonoid (Matrix n n R), docstring 'a square matrix is doubly stochastic if all entries are nonnegative, and left or right multiplication by the vector of all 1s gives the vector of all 1s.' This IS the standard definition of a doubly stochastic matrix.

Mathlib/Analysis/Complex/Trigonometric.lean:78-79 -- 'The real cosine function, defined as the real part of the complex cosine' / nonrec def cos (x : ℝ) : ℝ := (cos x).re, inside `namespace Real`. This IS the cosine function.

Archive/Sensitivity.lean:409-410 -- theorem huang_degree_theorem (H : Set (Q m.succ)) (hH : Card H ≥ 2^m+1) : ∃ q, q ∈ H ∧ √(m+1) ≤ Card H ∩ q.adjacent. File docstring: 'A formalization of Hao Huang's sensitivity theorem: in the hypercube of dimension n≥1, if one colors more than half the vertices then at least one vertex has at least √n colored neighbors.' This IS Huang's sensitivity theorem (resolving the Sensitivity Conjecture).

Mathlib/Algebra/Module/ZLattice/Basic.lean:435 -- class IsZLattice (K) {E} [NormedAddCommGroup E] [NormedSpace K E] (L : Submodule ℤ E): a full-rank discrete ℤ-submodule of a real (or general normed) vector space E. The concrete integer lattice ℤ^n ⊂ ℝ^n is the motivating/standard example (span of the standard basis) but Mathlib has no decl named specifically for ℤ^n itself; ZLattice/ZSpan is the strict general framework it instantiates.

Mathlib.Geometry.Euclidean.Triangle is a key in catalog/data/hierarchy.json; Mathlib/Geometry/Euclidean/Triangle.lean proves 'basic geometrical results about distances and angles in (possibly degenerate) triangles in real inner product spaces and Euclidean affine spaces' (law of cosines, law of sines, angle-sum, etc.) -- the closest dedicated formal home for the triangle-geometry subfield, alongside sibling files Circumcenter/Incenter/MongePoint/NinePointCircle/Altitude/PerpBisector in the same Geometry/Euclidean directory.
